records.ety: [OE. forbischen, OF. forbir, furbir, fourbir, F. fourbir, fr. OHG. furban to clean. See -ish.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Furbished (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Furbishing.]

defs: To rub or scour to brightness; to clean; to burnish; as, to furbish a sword or spear.
quotes: Furbish new the name of John a Gaunt.
